title: Why `podman` needs `dbus-user-session`?
body:
**Description**

In https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/12983#issuecomment-1019581809 `dbus-user-session` is needed to silence these `podman` warnings.

```bash
$ podman version
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.15
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

As I understand D-Bus is a desktop thing, and I am not planning to run any desktop apps on the remote server, so why do I need it to use `podman`?


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Get Debian 11 server
2. `apt install podman`
3. `podman version`

comments.body: Podman defaults to working with systemd for setting up cgroups and logging.  You can change your cgroup-manager and log-driver settings, and events log to cgroupfs, k8s-file, and file in containers.conf and that should remove the warnings.
